targets.sourceTitle: Syllabus Guidance Fall 2025 for Durham and Manchester

stopConditions: Stop after official UNH or USNH sources provide citation-ready evidence for AI-specific academic integrity, teaching guidance, privacy/security, and source-status claims.
Do not fetch robots-disallowed PDFs under /sites/default/files/*.pdf or login-gated SharePoint materials.

methodsAttempted.query: site:unh.edu University of New Hampshire generative AI policy ChatGPT academic integrity teaching guidance
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found the official Teaching & Learning Resource Hub generative AI page, Provost syllabus guidance, Academic Honesty hub, Faculty Senate AI report, and the catalog academic integrity policy.

methodsAttempted.query: site:unh.edu UNH generative AI approved tools privacy data security artificial intelligence ChatGPT
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found official career guidance, the Faculty Senate report, and scholarship PDFs; the USNH AI chatbot KB and AI standard were found by following official links from the Teaching & Learning Resource Hub and KB page.

methodsAttempted.query: site:unh.edu UNH artificial intelligence academic integrity automated writing tools syllabus guidance plagiarism
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search surfaced Provost syllabus guidance documents for Spring 2023 through Fall 2025 and Discovery template material. The current Fall 2025 Provost DOCX was used for the active guidance claim.

Robots.txt was checked for www.unh.edu, catalog.unh.edu, td.usnh.edu, and www.usnh.edu. The fetched HTML/DOCX/KB pages were allowed. www.unh.edu and www.usnh.edu disallow /sites/default/files/*.pdf, so official PDFs under that path were not fetched.

artifacts.summary: Official UNH and USNH discovery found current AI-relevant evidence in the Provost syllabus guidance, the UNH Catalog Academic Integrity policy, the USNH Artificial Intelligence Standard, the USNH AI Chatbots KB article, and UNH Teaching & Learning Resource Hub pages. The Faculty Senate report was treated as report-only context and not binding policy; career guidance and scholarship PDFs were rejected as low-specificity or research/showcase material.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Syllabus Guidance Fall 2025 for Durham and Manchester

artifacts.claimText: UNH Provost syllabus guidance recommends that instructors explicitly tell students whether automated writing tools and AI tools are allowed in a course, and if allowed, specify how and when they may be used.

citation.citationTitle: Syllabus Guidance Fall 2025 for Durham and Manchester
citation.publisher: University of New Hampshire Office of the Provost
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: UNH's Academic Integrity policy treats unauthorized use or attempted use of AI tools as cheating, and treats computer-generated text or work submitted as one's own without proper citation or attribution as plagiarism.

citation.publisher: University of New Hampshire Academic Catalog
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: UNH Provost syllabus guidance says generative-AI detection tools should not be used to make definitive academic-integrity judgments, and says uploading documents containing student names, emails, or ID numbers violates FERPA; faculty using AI-enabled tools to evaluate student work must disclose that use and keep student data protections in mind.

citation.citationTitle: Syllabus Guidance Fall 2025 for Durham and Manchester
citation.publisher: University of New Hampshire Office of the Provost
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: The USNH Artificial Intelligence Standard, which applies to USNH component institutions, says users of AI tools must not share or enter non-public Tier 2 or higher data, including PII or non-public research data, and that any AI implementation is subject to USNH policies, standards, and security review.

citation.publisher: University System of New Hampshire Enterprise Technology & Services
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: USNH's AI chatbot KB identifies DeepThought and Microsoft Copilot as USNH-provided AI options, recommends signing into Copilot with a USNH account for enterprise data protection, says public chatbots such as ChatGPT and Bard are not secure, and says alternative AI solutions must complete the Security Assessment Review process before purchase or implementation.

citation.publisher: University System of New Hampshire Knowledge Base
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: UNH maintains official Teaching & Learning Resource Hub pages for generative AI and academic honesty that point faculty to AI programming, UNH AI-related policies, the Provost syllabus guidance, and an Academic Honesty and AI policy summary; these pages were used as source-status context rather than binding policy by themselves.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Syllabus Guidance Fall 2025 for Durham and Manchester
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: The university recommends that instructors be very explicit with their students about whether any use of automated writing tools and tools that use artificial intelligence is allowed; and if allowed, be specific about just how and just when.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.citationTitle: Syllabus Guidance Fall 2025 for Durham and Manchester
citation.publisher: University of New Hampshire Office of the Provost
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Cheating: Use or attempted use of any academic exercise materials, information, study aids, electronic data, AI tools, assignment/exam surrogate, or other forms of assistance without authorization. Plagiarism: Use or submission of intellectual property, ideas, evidence produced by another person, including computer generated text or work outsourced to third-parties, in whole or in part as one's own in any academic assessment without providing proper citation or attribution.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.publisher: University of New Hampshire Academic Catalog
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Syllabus Guidance Fall 2025 for Durham and Manchester
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Turnitin and other software include features that analyze student work for potential plagiarism and use of generative AI tools... They should not be used to make definitive judgments about academic integrity. Further, uploading documents that contain student names, emails, or ID numbers constitutes a violation of FERPA. ChatGPT, Grammarly, Co-Pilot, and other tools may by useful for evaluating the content of student work. You must disclose your use to students.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.citationTitle: Syllabus Guidance Fall 2025 for Durham and Manchester
citation.publisher: University of New Hampshire Office of the Provost
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: This Standard applies to all administrative, academic, and business units at USNH and its component institutions. Users of AI tools or programs must not share or enter any data classified as non-public (Tier 2 data and above), including but not limited to Personally Identifiable information (PII) or non-public research data. Any implementation of artificial intelligence is subject to USNH policies and standards, including the security review process.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Sections 2 Scope, 3.1.2 Privacy and Data Protection, and 3.1.4 Accountability and Responsibility
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.publisher: University System of New Hampshire Enterprise Technology & Services
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: The platform includes several free language models (LLMs), which keep data private on the USNH network. Copilot is Microsoft's branded chatbot... We recommend signing in with your USNH account to benefit from enterprise data protection. Public platforms like ChatGPT, Bard and others are not secure and pose risks related to data privacy, intellectual property and compliance with cybersecurity standards. Any alternative must successfully complete the Security Assessment Review (SAR) process before purchase or implementation.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: DeepThought, Copilot, and Privacy considerations for public chatbots sections

artifacts.notes: Approved as a guidance claim only; wording uses recommends and does not treat sample syllabus language as a binding universal prohibition.
Approved because the current UNH Catalog policy explicitly names AI tools under cheating and computer-generated text under plagiarism.
Approved as Provost syllabus guidance scoped to detection/evaluation and student-data handling.
Approved because the USNH standard explicitly applies to component institutions and uses mandatory non-public-data and security-review language.
Approved as a USNH tool-treatment and security-process claim; does not imply all tools are approved for all data tiers.
Approved only as source-status context for official UNH AI teaching resources, not as a binding policy claim.

artifacts.title: University of New Hampshire AI Policy Evidence Draft
artifacts.summary: Draft evidence bundle for University of New Hampshire based on official UNH and USNH sources. It includes current catalog academic-integrity language covering AI tools and computer-generated text, Provost syllabus guidance for course AI expectations and FERPA-sensitive AI use, USNH cybersecurity AI standard requirements, USNH chatbot/tool guidance, and source-status context from UNH Teaching & Learning Resource Hub pages.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The Faculty Senate AI committee report was not used as policy evidence because it is report context and the PDF path is robots-disallowed.
SharePoint workshop handouts linked from UNH pages were not fetched because they may require access controls or tenant context.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
